Abstract   
Agricultural productivity is strongly influenced by environmental conditions and the timely 
detection of crop diseases. Traditional farming methods largely rely on manual inspection, 
which often delays the identification of soil moisture deficiency and vis ible disease symptoms. 
Such delays can lead to inefficient irr

raditional farming methods largely rely on manual inspection, 
which often delays the identification of soil moisture deficiency and vis ible disease symptoms. 
Such delays can lead to inefficient irrigation, reduced crop yield, and economic losses. To 
address these challenges, this study proposes an AI -enabled IoT system for smart  crop 
monitoring and disease detection that integrates real -time environmental sensing with 
intelligent image -based disease analysis.   
The proposed system utilizes an  ESP32 microcontroller connected to a YL -69 soil moisture 
sensor and a DS18B20 temperature sensor to continuously monitor soil and environmental 
conditions. The collected data is transmitted to a cloud platform for remote monitoring and 
storage. In addition , a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) model is implemented to analyze 
plant leaf images and classify them as healthy or diseased.

However, modern agriculture constantly faces challenges such as climate change, 
unpredictable rainfall patterns, soil degradation, and the rapid spread of crop di seases. 
Conventional agricultural systems rely mostly on manual field observation, where farmers

2.1 Existing System   
Existing smart agriculture systems primarily focus on either environmental monitoring or crop 
disease detection as separate solutions. Several IoT -based systems utilize soil moisture and 
temperature sensors to monitor field conditions and assist in irrigat ion management. These 
systems transmit sensor data to cloud platforms fo

s. Several IoT -based systems utilize soil moisture and 
temperature sensors to monitor field conditions and assist in irrigat ion management. These 
systems transmit sensor data to cloud platforms for remote monitoring. However, most of 
these solutions are limited to environmental parameter tracking and do not incorporate 
intelligent disease detection mechanisms.   
Similarly, AI -based crop disease detection systems have been developed using machine 
learning and deep learning techniques such as Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN). These 
systems analyses the plant leaf images to classify diseases with reasonable accura cy. 
However, they typically operate independently of environmental sensing modules and lack 
real-time integration with field conditions. As a result, existing systems often address isolated 
aspects of precision agriculture rather than providing a unified, integrated smart monitoring 
framework.

2.2 Proposed System   
The proposed AI -Enabled IoT System for Smart Crop Monitoring and Disease Detection 
integrates environmental monitoring and intelligent disease analysis into a single unified 
platform. The system consists of  ESP32 microcontroller connected to a YL -69 soil moisture 
sensor and a DS18B20 temperature sensor for continuous real -time data acquisition. Sensor 
readings are transmitted to a cloud platform for remote monitoring and storage.   
In addition to that a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) model is implemented for 
automated disease detection using plant leaf images. Unlike existing standalone solutions, the 
proposed system combines Both the IoT -based environmental sensing and AI -driven  image 
classification to provide real -time monitoring, threshold -based alerts, and decision support. 
This integrated approac

ystem combines Both the IoT -based environmental sensing and AI -driven  image 
classification to provide real -time monitoring, threshold -based alerts, and decision support. 
This integrated approach enhances irrigation efficiency, enables early disease detection, and 
improves overall precision agriculture practices.

3. Methodology   
  
The proposed AI -Enabled IoT System for Smart Crop Monitoring and Disease Detection 
combines environmental sensing with AI -driven image analysis. The hardware components 
consist of an ESP32 microcontroller interfaced with a YL -69 soil moisture sensor and a 
DS18B20 temperature sensor. The ESP32 is programmed using the Arduino IDE to read 
realtime sensor data and upload it to a cloud platform over Wi -Fi.   
The system uses threshold logic to detect soil moisture levels and send notifications when the 
values exceed or falls below the set thresholds. Moreover, the system analyz

ud platform over Wi -Fi.   
The system uses threshold logic to detect soil moisture levels and send notifications when the 
values exceed or falls below the set thresholds. Moreover, the system analyzes plant leaf 
images using a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) model to classify cro ps as healthy or 
diseased. The system is tested to ensure the accuracy of the sensors, communication 
reliability, and disease detection capability. This approach combines IoT -based crop 
monitoring with AI -driven analysis to enable precision agriculture.   
   
  
  
  

  
3.1. Sensors Working Principle   
  
The sensors work by continuously reading data and processing it using the ESP32 
microcontroller. The YL -69 soil moisture sensor reads soil conductivity, which is proportional 
to soil moisture, and gives analog output readings that are processed by the ESP3 2 
microcontroller. The DS18B20 temperature sensor reads temperature digitally using a onewire 
communication protocol and gives stable a

nd gives analog output readings that are processed by the ESP3 2 
microcontroller. The DS18B20 temperature sensor reads temperature digitally using a onewire 
communication protocol and gives stable and accurate readings.   
The ESP32 microcontroller processes the sensor readings based on set thresholds to decide 
irrigation schedules. At the same time, the CNN model analyzes plant leaf images to classify 
crops as healthy or diseased. The system combines real -time environmental  sensing with 
AIdriven image analysis to enable smart farming decisions.

5. Conclusion and Future Scope   
In summary, the design and deployment of the AI -Enabled IoT System for Smart Crop  
Monitoring and Disease Detection is a major breakthrough in the field of precision 
agriculture. The combination of IoT -based environmental monitoring and AI -assisted disease 
diagnosis makes the system a complete solution for real -time crop monitoring, effi cient 
irrigation management, and early disease detection in crops. The continuous monitoring of 
soil moisture and temperature using the ESP32 microcontroller enables the sys tem to make 
informed

i cient 
irrigation management, and early disease detection in crops. The continuous monitoring of 
soil moisture and temperature using the ESP32 microcontroller enables the sys tem to make 
informed decisions, while the CNN -based image classification model enables the system to 
make timely decisions to minimize crop damage. The system improves efficiency, 
optimization, and sustainability in modern agriculture.   
There are many opportunities in the future to further improve the capabilities of the system. 
The system can be improved by incorporating other environmental sensors such as humidity 
and soil nutrient sensors for more detailed analysis of the field. The sy stem can be further 
improved by incorporating automated irrigation control systems based on real -time sensor 
readings. The system can also be improved by incorporating more advanced AI models with 
larger datasets and deep learning models for more accurate disease classification. Cloudbased 
analytics and predictive

. The system can also be improved by incorporating more advanced AI models with 
larger datasets and deep learning models for more accurate disease classification. Cloudbased 
analytics and predictive modeling can also be incorporated into the system to provide 
advanced decision support and long -term crop performance analysis. In summary, the 
proposed system has immense potential for large -scale, intelligent, and sustainable smart 
farming.

combines environmental sensing with AI -driven image analysis. The hardware components 
consist of an ESP32 microcontroller interfaced with a YL -69 soil moisture sensor and a 
DS18B20 temperature sensor. The ESP32 is programmed using the Arduino IDE to read 
realtime sensor data and upload it to a cloud platform over Wi -Fi.

values exceed or falls below the set thresholds. Moreover, the system analyz

Abstract   
Agricultural productivity is strongly influenced by environmental conditions and the timely 
detection of crop diseases. Traditional farming methods largely rely on manual inspection, 
which often delays the identification of soil moisture deficiency and vis ible disease symptoms. 
Such delays can lead to inefficient irr

raditional farming methods largely rely on manual inspection, 
which often delays the identification of soil moisture deficiency and vis ible disease symptoms. 
Such delays can lead to inefficient irrigation, reduced crop yield, and economic losses. To 
address these challenges, this study proposes an AI -enabled IoT system for smart  crop 
monitoring and disease detection that integrates real -time environmental sensing with 
intelligent image -based disease analysis.   
The proposed system utilizes an  ESP32 microcontroller connected to a YL -69 soil moisture 
sensor and a DS18B20 temperature sensor to continuously monitor soil and environmental 
conditions. The collected data is transmitted to a cloud platform for remote monitoring and 
storage. In addition , a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) model is implemented to analyze 
plant leaf images and classify them as healthy or diseased.

Agricultural productivity is strongly influenced by environmental conditions and the timely 
detection of crop diseases. Traditional farming methods largely rely on manual inspection, 
which often delays the identification of soil moisture deficiency and vis ible disease symptoms. 
Such delays can lead to inefficient irr

raditional farming methods largely rely on manual inspection, 
which often delays the identification of soil moisture deficiency and vis ible disease symptoms. 
Such delays can lead to inefficient irrigation, reduced crop yield, and economic losses. To 
address these challenges, this study proposes an AI -enabled IoT system for smart  crop

Abstract   
Agricultural productivity is strongly influenced by environmental conditions and the timely 
detection of crop diseases. Traditional farming methods largely rely on manual inspection, 
which often delays the identification of soil moisture deficiency and vis ible disease symptoms. 
Such delays can lead to inefficient irr

raditional farming methods largely rely on manual inspection, 
which often delays the identification of soil moisture deficiency and vis ible disease symptoms. 
Such delays can lead to inefficient irrigation, reduced crop yield, and economic losses. To 
address these challenges, this study proposes an AI -enabled IoT system for smart  crop 
monitoring and disease detection that integrates real -time environmental sensing with 
intelligent image -based disease analysis.   
The proposed system utilizes an  ESP32 microcontroller connected to a YL -69 soil moisture 
sensor and a DS18B20 temperature sensor to continuously monitor soil and environmental 
conditions. The collected data is transmitted to a cloud platform for remote monitoring and 
storage. In addition , a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) model is implemented to analyze 
plant leaf images and classify them as healthy or diseased.

Prototype testing demonstrates stable sensor data acquisition, reliable wireless communication, 
and effective disease classification performance. By combining IoT -based monitoring with 
AIdriven analysis, the system enhances precision agriculture practices,  optimizes water 
management, and enables early decision -making for sustainable and efficient farming.

3. Methodology   
  
The proposed AI -Enabled IoT System for Smart Crop Monitoring and Disease Detection 
combines environmental sensing with AI -driven image analysis. The hardware components 
consist of an ESP32 microcontroller interfaced with a YL -69 soil moisture sensor and a 
DS18B20 temperature sensor. The ESP32 is programmed using the Arduino IDE to read 
realtime sensor data and upload it to a cloud platform over Wi -Fi.   
The system uses threshold logic to detect soil moisture levels and send notifications when the 
values exceed or falls below the set thresholds. Moreover, the system analyz

ud platform over Wi -Fi.   
The system uses threshold logic to detect soil moisture levels and send notifications when the 
values exceed or falls below the set thresholds. Moreover, the system analyzes plant leaf 
images using a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) model to classify cro ps as healthy or 
diseased. The system is tested to ensure the accuracy of the sensors, communication 
reliability, and disease detection capability. This approach combines IoT -based crop 
monitoring with AI -driven analysis to enable precision agriculture.

The sensors work by continuously reading data and processing it using the ESP32 
microcontroller. The YL -69 soil moisture sensor reads soil conductivity, which is proportional 
to soil moisture, and gives analog output readings that are processed by the ESP3 2 
microcontroller. The DS18B20 temperature sensor reads temperature digitally using a onewire 
communication protocol and gives stable a

nd gives analog output readings that are processed by the ESP3 2 
microcontroller. The DS18B20 temperature sensor reads temperature digitally using a onewire 
communication protocol and gives stable and accurate readings.   
The ESP32 microcontroller processes the sensor readings based on set thresholds to decide 
irrigation schedules. At the same time, the CNN model analyzes plant leaf images to classify 
crops as healthy or diseased. The system combines real -time environmental  sensing with 
AIdriven image analysis to enable smart farming decisions.

Abstract   
Agricultural productivity is strongly influenced by environmental conditions and the timely 
detection of crop diseases. Traditional farming methods largely rely on manual inspection, 
which often delays the identification of soil moisture deficiency and vis ible disease symptoms. 
Such delays can lead to inefficient irr

raditional farming methods largely rely on manual inspection, 
which often delays the identification of soil moisture deficiency and vis ible disease symptoms. 
Such delays can lead to inefficient irrigation, reduced crop yield, and economic losses. To 
address these challenges, this study proposes an AI -enabled IoT system for smart  crop 
monitoring and disease detection that integrates real -time environmental sensing with 
intelligent image -based disease analysis.   
The proposed system utilizes an  ESP32 microcontroller connected to a YL -69 soil moisture 
sensor and a DS18B20 temperature sensor to continuously monitor soil and environmental 
conditions. The collected data is transmitted to a cloud platform for remote monitoring and 
storage. In addition , a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) model is implemented to analyze 
plant leaf images and classify them as healthy or diseased.

3. Methodology   
  
The proposed AI -Enabled IoT System for Smart Crop Monitoring and Disease Detection 
combines environmental sensing with AI -driven image analysis. The hardware components 
consist of an ESP32 microcontroller interfaced with a YL -69 soil moisture sensor and a 
DS18B20 temperature sensor. The ESP32 is programmed using the Arduino IDE to read 
realtime sensor data and upload it to a cloud platform over Wi -Fi.   
The system uses threshold logic to detect soil moisture levels and send notifications when the 
values exceed or falls below the set thresholds. Moreover, the system analyz

ud platform over Wi -Fi.   
The system uses threshold logic to detect soil moisture levels and send notifications when the 
values exceed or falls below the set thresholds. Moreover, the system analyzes plant leaf 
images using a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) model to classify cro ps as healthy or 
diseased. The system is tested to ensure the accuracy of the sensors, communication 
reliability, and disease detection capability. This approach combines IoT -based crop 
monitoring with AI -driven analysis to enable precision agriculture.

The sensors work by continuously reading data and processing it using the ESP32 
microcontroller. The YL -69 soil moisture sensor reads soil conductivity, which is proportional 
to soil moisture, and gives analog output readings that are processed by the ESP3 2 
microcontroller. The DS18B20 temperature sensor reads temperature digitally using a onewire 
communication protocol and gives stable a

nd gives analog output readings that are processed by the ESP3 2 
microcontroller. The DS18B20 temperature sensor reads temperature digitally using a onewire 
communication protocol and gives stable and accurate readings.   
The ESP32 microcontroller processes the sensor readings based on set thresholds to decide 
irrigation schedules. At the same time, the CNN model analyzes plant leaf images to classify 
crops as healthy or diseased. The system combines real -time environmental  sensing with 
AIdriven image analysis to enable smart farming decisions.

The document proposes an AI-enabled IoT system for smart crop monitoring and disease detection. This system addresses challenges in traditional farming, such as delayed detection of soil moisture deficiency and crop diseases, which lead to inefficient irrigation, reduced crop yield, and economic losses.The proposed system integrates real-time environmental sensing with intelligent image-based disease analysis. It uses an ESP32 microcontroller connected to a YL-69 soil moisture sensor and a DS18B20 temperature sensor to continuously monitor soil and environmental conditions. The collected data is transmitted to a cloud platform for remote monitoring and storage. Additionally, a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) model is implemented to analyze plant leaf images and classify them as healthy or diseased.Prototype testing demonstrated stable sensor data acquisition, reliable wireless communication, and effective disease classification performance.
